dialogueChunks.intro: {player_name}, the situation at {location} has grown dire. {quest_ally} sent word that the {enemy_type} have doubled their assault. The defenders are holding, but barely.
dialogueChunks.plotStep: The commander has ordered the youngest soldiers to fall back while the veterans hold the line. They know they're buying time with their lives. {target_npc} tried to convince them to retreat together, but they refused: said the ideal they swore to protect is bigger than any one of them. They need {quest_item} to shore up their defenses.
dialogueChunks.outro: Every hour those defenders hold saves dozens of lives downstream. If you can get {quest_item} to them, it might turn hours into days.

playerResponses.text: I'll get it there. Their sacrifice won't be in vain.
Is there no way to get reinforcements instead?
They've chosen their fate. I won't share it.

dialogueChunks.intro: One of the defenders broke. Ran from the post in the night. Can't say I blame them, but now there's a gap that needs filling.
dialogueChunks.plotStep: {target_npc} caught the deserter {location_hint} and talked them down. They weren't a coward: they were a parent. Three children waiting at home and a post that demanded they never come back. {quest_ally} is trying to redistribute the remaining fighters, but without {quest_item}, the defensive line has a hole that {quest_threat} will pour through.
dialogueChunks.outro: The ideal asks everything. Most people can give almost everything. That last fraction is where heroes are made, or broken.

playerResponses.text: I'll fill the gap. Someone has to.
The deserter had the right idea. These people need to retreat.

dialogueChunks.intro: The healer inside {quest_focus} is weakening. She's been exposed for days now and her hands shake when she mixes the remedies. But she won't stop.
dialogueChunks.plotStep: {target_npc} says she needs one more batch of {quest_item} to finish treating the last patients. After that, whether she survives depends on how much damage the sickness has already done to her. She knows this. She told {quest_ally} through the wall: 'The oath doesn't have an exception clause.'
dialogueChunks.outro: Find {quest_item} and bring it to {location}. If the healer finishes her work, every life in that ward is saved. The only life in question is hers.

playerResponses.text: She deserves every chance to survive this. I'll find what she needs.
Has anyone tried to talk her out of there?
I'm not sure more supplies will change the outcome.
